Tests if the typeof operator correctly identifies numbers.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion script that checks the data type of two numeric literals. It uses the `assert` function to verify that both the integer `37` and the floating-point number `3.14` are of the type `number`. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing solely on confirming the type of these specific numeric values. It is likely used in a testing context to ensure that the JavaScript environment correctly identifies numeric types.
